Dear Diary,

School started today and it was SO dumb. I'm in 6th grade now which means I have a locker and different teachers for every class, it's very confusing. Sarah K.

*September 14, 1996*

Dear Diary,

I'm so mad. Mom dropped me off at school today and Jenny was waiting for me at the front doors and she said "did you hear about Kaitlin?" and I said no and she said Kaitlin Miller got her period over the summer and she told everyone at lunch. S.

Dear Diary,

Jenny's mom took us to the mall today and we went to The Limited Too and I got a new top that's kind of sparkly and Jenny got a skirt and then we went to the food court and we saw Travis Brewer and Cody at the arcade and they came over and Travis sat down next to me and I didn't know what to do with my face.

He said "cool shirt" and I said "thanks" and then I took a sip of my Orange Julius because I needed something to do with my hands and I think I looked really calm on the outside but on the inside my whole chest felt like a TV with no signal, just the staticky snow, all buzzing and no picture.

Then we left and in the car Jenny said "he totally likes you" and I said "no he doesn't, he's mean" and she said "he's not mean, he's a boy" which is maybe the same thing?? But I didn't say that.

S.

Dear Diary,

After school I was watching Full House and Michelle said something about liking a boy and Dad changed the channel and I didn't say anything and he changed it back after like 2 minutes but I could tell he thought it was "too old" for me. It's not. I'm not a baby.

Secret: I think about Travis a lot. Not like in a weird way, just like I wonder what he's doing right now, or what he thought about the homework, or whether he'll sit by me at lunch tomorrow. Is that weird? It probably is.

S.

*October 31, 1996*

Dear Diary,

Happy Halloween!! I went as a witch this year, Jenny and I matched, we made the hats out of construction paper and they kept falling off. We went trick-or-treating around my neighborhood and then Jenny's mom picked us up at 8:30.

We saw some high school boys sitting on a porch and one of them said "nice costumes girls" in a way that felt weird in my stomach but also kind of good?? I don't know. I didn't say anything back. Jenny said "thanks" but quietly, like she wasn't sure either.

Dear Diary,

I had a dream about Travis last night. We were in the cafeteria and he asked me to pass the ketchup and when I handed it to him our fingers touched and then I woke up but my whole body felt like it was humming, like a radio that's between stations, staticky and warm.

Is that what a crush feels like? I've been calling it that in my head but I don't really know. Jenny says she has a crush on Jonathan Taylor Thomas but that's different, he's on TV. Travis is HERE, in my school, in my homeroom, breathing the same air as me. It feels more real. More scary.

S.

*November 27, 1996*

Dear Diary,

Tomorrow is Thanksgiving! Grandma's coming here which is okay I guess. She always brings her lemon bars which are kind of gross but I eat one to be polite. Mom and Dad have been cooking all day and the house smells really good, like turkey and stuffing.

I helped Mom make the cranberry sauce and she let me stir it on the stove and I felt kind of grown-up, like we were doing something together that was real and not just her supervising me. She put her hand on my shoulder for a second while I was stirring and said "you're getting so tall" and I didn't know what to say so I just shrugged.

Jenny called tonight to ask what I'm wearing tomorrow. I said jeans and my green sweater and she said she's wearing a dress because her grandma is "fancy" which made me laugh. I'm glad we're friends.

S.

*December 3, 1996*

Dear Diary,

Something weird happened today. I was laying in bed last night and I couldn't sleep and I was thinking about the dream I had about Travis, about his fingers touching mine, and my brain kept going to this place that felt kind of like sneezing but lower, like in my stomach, and before I knew it my hand was drifting down and I was pressing against my pajama bottoms right THERE and it felt... good? I stopped after a minute because I felt weird doing it even though nobody could see me. It's not like anyone TOLD me to do it, my hand just went there on its own. I pulled it back up and lay there with my eyes wide open for a long time.

Is that a thing people do? I feel like it's a thing people do. I don't know why I feel like I can't ask anyone about it. Maybe Jenny would know. Or maybe that would be weird. Maybe that would be the weirdest thing I've ever asked her and she'd tell everyone.

S.

*December 10, 1996*

Dear Diary,

Jenny and I are in a fight. Well not a FIGHT fight, but she's been hanging out with Kaitlin more at lunch and today she sat with her instead of me and when I asked her about it after school she said "it's not a big deal, I can have other friends" which made me feel like I got punched in the ribs.

S.

**SARAH'S DIARY**

*December 17, 1996*

Dear Diary,

*December 22, 1996*

Dear Diary,

Last day of school before break!! We had a party in homeroom and Mrs. Reese let us have cookies and watch a movie, it was Home Alone 2 which I've already seen but it was still fun. *December 28, 1996*

Dear Diary,

I did it three more times since I last wrote. Three. Every time I tell myself I'm not going to and then night comes and I can't sleep and my brain goes back to that feeling and my hand just goes there.

Today I noticed that when I was watching TV and a cute boy came on screen I felt kind of tingly. Not the big feeling, just a little version of it, like my body was remembering what it could do. Is that normal? Is my body going to start doing this ALL THE TIME now? That seems really inconvinient (inconvienent?? I don't know how to spell that).

S.

Dear Diary,

And it felt really good, better than when I'm awake even, because I wasn't thinking about whether I should or shouldn't, my hand was just moving in a rhythm and it felt warm and electric and then the big feeling happened but this time it was stronger and I think I made a noise?? A little one. And my hips kind of moved on their own.

What is HAPPENING to me.

I looked at myself in the mirror this morning, right after, and my face was all pink and my eyes looked different, bigger maybe. I looked like someone I didn't know. Like a girl from a movie about older teenagers, not like me.

I don't think I can stop. I don't even know if I want to. I just wish I knew what it WAS.

S.

Dear Diary,

School started again. Boring. Everyone kept talking about what they got for Christmas and I didn't get anything that exciting, just clothes and a CD player which is actually great but Jenny got a phone in her room so obviously that's more exciting.

S.

I don't know why my brain decided to put that image where it keeps the other images, the Travis ones, the ones that make me feel tingly.

It's probably nothing. She's my best friend. I probably just noticed because I notice everything lately. My body is like a radio that won't turn off, just scanning through stations, picking up static and signals from everywhere.

S.
